pois.name: Magdeburger Dom
pois.description: The Cathedral of Saints Maurice and Catherine is the oldest Gothic cathedral on German soil. It houses the tomb of Emperor Otto the Great and serves as the landmark of the state capital.

pois.name: Naumburger Dom St. Peter und Paul
pois.description: A UNESCO World Heritage site famous for the Naumburg Master's donor figures, including Uta von Ballenstedt. It represents a unique transition from late Romanesque to early Gothic architecture.

pois.name: Schloss Wernigerode
pois.description: Originally a medieval fortress, this castle was transformed into a representative palace in the 19th century. It overlooks the town of Wernigerode and the northern Harz landscape.

pois.name: Altstadt Quedlinburg
pois.description: A exceptionally well-preserved medieval and Renaissance town with over 2,000 half-timbered houses. The ensemble is a UNESCO World Heritage site and was a center of the Ottonian dynasty.

pois.name: Schlosskirche Wittenberg
pois.description: The site where Martin Luther is said to have posted his 95 theses in 1517, sparking the Protestant Reformation. The church also serves as the final resting place for Luther and Melanchthon.

pois.name: Burg Falkenstein
pois.description: One of the most impressive and best-preserved high medieval castles in the Harz region. It is believed to be the place where Eike von Repgow wrote the Sachsenspiegel law code.

pois.name: Kloster Unser Lieben Frauen
pois.description: The oldest building in Magdeburg, this former Premonstratensian monastery is a major example of Romanesque architecture. It now serves as a museum of contemporary art and a concert hall.

pois.name: Sonnenobservatorium Goseck
pois.description: Europe's oldest known circular solar observatory, reconstructed on its original site. The henge structure dates back to the Neolithic period and aligns with the winter solstice.

pois.name: Teufelsmauer Weddersleben
pois.description: A striking sandstone ridge stretching for several kilometers. The 'Devil's Wall' at Weddersleben is the most famous part of this geological monument formed in the Cretaceous period.

pois.name: Altstadt Tangermünde
pois.description: A historic Hanseatic city on the Elbe River, known for its nearly complete city wall and red brick Gothic architecture. It was once the secondary residence of Emperor Charles IV.

pois.name: Rudelsburg
pois.description: A medieval hill castle ruin overlooking the Saale valley. It became a popular destination during the Romantic period and a focal point for German student fraternities.

pois.name: Schloss Neuenburg
pois.description: A massive castle complex built by the Landgraves of Thuringia. It features a rare Romanesque double chapel and overlooks the wine-growing region of Saale-Unstrut.

pois.name: Kloster Pforta
pois.description: A former Cistercian monastery that later became a prestigious boarding school. Its alumni include famous figures like Friedrich Nietzsche and Leopold von Ranke.

pois.name: Merseburger Dom
pois.description: Known for the Merseburg Incantations, two of the oldest surviving Germanic pagan spells. The cathedral site has been a major religious center since the 10th century.

pois.name: Ringheiligtum Pömmelte
pois.description: A reconstructed prehistoric woodhenge structure used for astronomical observation and ritual ceremonies. It is often described as the German counterpart to Stonehenge.

pois.name: Arche Nebra
pois.description: The visitor center located near the discovery site of the Nebra Sky Disc, the world's oldest concrete depiction of the stars. It provides insights into Bronze Age astronomy.

pois.name: Halberstädter Dom
pois.description: A high Gothic cathedral famous for its exceptionally preserved medieval treasury. The collection includes ancient textiles and reliquaries unique in Europe.

pois.name: Stiftskirche St. Cyriakus
pois.description: One of the best-preserved examples of Ottonian architecture in Germany. The church in Gernrode features an early Romanesque Holy Sepulchre and a historic Westwerk.

pois.name: Schloss Bernburg
pois.description: A Renaissance palace situated on a limestone cliff above the Saale river. It is known for its 'bear pit' and its connection to the Princes of Anhalt-Bernburg.

pois.name: Altstadt Stolberg
pois.description: A historic town in the Harz mountains known for its uniform half-timbered architecture. It is the birthplace of the reformer Thomas Müntzer and the site of a grand palace.

pois.name: Burgruine Regenstein
pois.description: A unique rock fortress where many rooms and corridors were carved directly into the sandstone cliffs. It offers panoramic views of the northern Harz foreland.

pois.name: Brockengipfel
pois.description: The highest peak in the Harz Mountains and Northern Germany. Steeped in legends of witches and Walpurgis Night, it was a restricted military zone during the Cold War.

pois.name: Rosstrappe
pois.description: A prominent granite rock high above the Bode Gorge. Legend says the 'hoofprint' in the rock was made by the horse of Princess Brunhilde as she jumped across the valley.

pois.name: Hexentanzplatz Thale
pois.description: An ancient cult site and plateau above the Bode valley. Traditionally associated with Walpurgis Night, it now features an open-air theater and sculptures of mythic creatures.

pois.name: Havelberger Dom
pois.description: A Romanesque and Gothic cathedral that served as the center of the Diocese of Havelberg. It marks the northernmost tip of the Romanesque Road in Saxony-Anhalt.

pois.name: Schloss Moritzburg Zeitz
pois.description: An early Baroque palace built for the Dukes of Saxe-Zeitz. It houses the German Carriage Museum and an extensive collection of historic baby carriages.

pois.name: Kloster Memleben
pois.description: The site of a former Imperial Abbey and the place where King Henry the Fowler and Emperor Otto the Great died. Only ruins of the monumental 13th-century church remain.

pois.name: Burg Querfurt
pois.description: One of the largest medieval castles in Germany, featuring three massive towers and extensive fortifications. It is often used as a filming location for historical movies.

pois.name: Schloss Köthen
pois.description: A former residence of the Princes of Anhalt-Köthen. Johann Sebastian Bach worked here as Kapellmeister, composing many of his famous instrumental works.

pois.name: Arendsee
pois.description: The largest and deepest natural lake in Saxony-Anhalt, formed by the collapse of an underground salt dome. It is a popular recreation spot in the Altmark region.

pois.name: Lutherhaus Wittenberg
pois.description: The world's largest museum of Reformation history, located in the former Augustinian monastery where Martin Luther lived for most of his life.

pois.name: Kloster Drübeck
pois.description: A former Benedictine nunnery along the Romanesque Road. It is famous for its historic gardens and the 1,000-year-old linden tree on the monastery grounds.

pois.name: Kloster Michaelstein
pois.description: A former Cistercian monastery near Blankenburg. Today it is an academy for music and features a unique garden with medieval herbs and medicinal plants.

pois.name: Moritzburg Halle
pois.description: A late Gothic castle that served as the residence of the Archbishops of Magdeburg. It now houses the Art Museum of the State of Saxony-Anhalt.

pois.name: Schloss Wörlitz
pois.description: The first Neoclassical palace in Germany, located in the Wörlitz Garden Realm. It was built for Prince Leopold III Friedrich Franz of Anhalt-Dessau.

pois.name: Josephskreuz
pois.description: The world's largest iron double cross, located on the Auerberg. It is a 38-meter tall observation tower designed in the style of a medieval framework tower.

pois.name: Königshütter Wasserfall
pois.description: An artificial but picturesque waterfall in the Harz mountains, created during former mining operations. It is located near the confluence of the Cold and Warm Bode rivers.

pois.name: Altstadt Werben
pois.description: One of Germany's smallest Hanseatic cities, known for its red brick Gothic gate and the massive St. Johannis church. It is often called the 'Stork City'.

pois.name: Kugelbrücke Quedlinburg
pois.description: A small historic stone bridge in the old town of Quedlinburg, crossing the Bode river. It represents typical late medieval infrastructure in the merchant district.

pois.name: Marktkirche Unser Lieben Frauen
pois.description: Located in the center of Halle, this church features four towers that dominate the skyline. Martin Luther preached here several times, and George Frideric Handel was baptized here.

pois.name: Luthers Geburtshaus
pois.description: The house in Eisleben where Martin Luther was born in 1483. It is one of the oldest museums in the German-speaking world and a UNESCO World Heritage site.

pois.name: Kloster Huysburg
pois.description: A Benedictine monastery located on the Huy ridge. It survived the Reformation and secularization and remains an active religious center with a significant Romanesque church.

pois.name: Burgruine Giebichenstein
pois.description: A castle ruin on a cliff above the Saale in Halle. It was an important residency for the Archbishops of Magdeburg and now houses part of the local art academy.

pois.name: Schloss Allstedt
pois.description: A former Imperial Palace where Thomas Müntzer delivered his famous 'Sermon to the Princes'. It was also a favorite retreat for Johann Wolfgang von Goethe.

pois.name: Europa-Rosarium
pois.description: The world's largest collection of roses, located in Sangerhausen. It preserves over 8,600 varieties and serves as a vital genetic database for the genus Rosa.

pois.name: Hamburger Wappen
pois.description: A distinct group of vertical sandstone rocks near Timmenrode, part of the Teufelsmauer ridge. Its three pinnacles resemble the coat of arms of the city of Hamburg.

pois.name: Schloss Hundisburg
pois.description: One of the most important Baroque palace complexes in Saxony-Anhalt, featuring a large landscaped garden and an extensive park designed in the French and English styles.

pois.name: Stendaler Roland
pois.description: A monumental stone statue in front of the town hall, symbolizing the city's rights and freedoms. At 7.8 meters, it is one of the tallest Roland statues in Germany.

pois.name: Kaiserpfalz Tilleda
pois.description: An open-air museum at the site of a fully excavated medieval Imperial Palace. It shows the structure of an Ottonian and Staufian residence at the foot of the Kyffhäuser.

pois.name: St. Stephani Aschersleben
pois.description: A large Gothic hall church that dominates the skyline of the oldest town in Saxony-Anhalt. It houses a rare late medieval library and a Craneach altar.

pois.name: Burg Saaleck
pois.description: The ruin of a medieval hilltop castle near the Rudelsburg. It is notable for its two distinctive round keep towers and its dramatic position overlooking the Saale valley.

pois.name: Schloss Oranienbaum
pois.description: A Dutch-style Baroque palace and town ensemble built for Princess Henriette Catharina of Nassau-Orange. It features a unique Chinese garden and pagoda.

pois.name: Gradierwerk Bad Kösen
pois.description: A 325-meter long salt graduation tower used for brine extraction and inhalation therapy. It is a masterpiece of industrial technology from the mid-18th century.

pois.name: Kloster Jerichow
pois.description: A prime example of early brick Romanesque architecture in Northern Germany. The former Premonstratensian abbey remains almost unchanged since its construction.

pois.name: Schloss Mosigkau
pois.description: Often called the 'Little Sanssouci', this Rococo palace was built as a summer residence for Anna Wilhelmine of Anhalt-Dessau. It contains a rare collection of Dutch paintings.

pois.name: Schloss Pretzsch
pois.description: A Renaissance palace on the Elbe River that served as a dower house for the Electresses of Saxony. It is closely linked to the history of the Electress Christiane Eberhardine.

pois.name: Wippraer Museumsbrauerei
pois.description: A traditional brewery in the Harz mountains that still uses open fermentation and classic methods. It maintains a museum showing the history of beer production.

pois.name: Historischer Gasthof Zum Bären
pois.description: One of the oldest inns in Quedlinburg, operating since the 18th century. The building itself is an architectural treasure with detailed carvings and historic interiors.

pois.name: Ferropolis
pois.description: The 'City of Iron' is an open-air museum featuring five massive decommissioned industrial excavators. It serves as a monument to the region's lignite mining history.

pois.name: Eckartsburg
pois.description: A medieval castle overlooking the town of Eckartsberga. It was an important military post on the Via Regia and features a well-preserved square keep.
